steps.text: If a CIO makes a determination that there is a potential conflict of interest concern, then the CIO must first consult with OGC.
The CIO can request a CDC COI Panel Review if it requires further elevation.
When the Emergency Operations Center (EOC) is activated for a public health emergency, follow the emergency response conflict of interest review process.
This process includes a review from OGC and the Ethics Desk of the EOC.
Complete a Gift Acceptance Validity Survey and relay further considerations back to OGC, the Ethics Desk, and the CDC Foundation.
Gifts offered by a prohibited source must undergo an extra layer of scrutiny.
Completion of the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey is required before possibly accepting a gift by CDC.
Do not accept gifts tendered to secure an implied or direct endorsement.
Advise donors from commercial organizations that acceptance of a gift does not imply endorsement.
Do not accept gifts when the donor expects a future benefit.
Only the Secretary, HHS/ASFR can accept conditional gifts of real property.
Use gift funds to further authorized agency purposes and in accordance with the terms of the donation.
Evaluate the propriety of proposed uses of gift funds against the agency's authorizing statute.
Use gift funds to pay federal employee salary and benefits if conditions do not create a conflict of interest.
Pay only the salary of term employees using gift funds.
Forward the cash or cash equivalents for deposit to the Deputy Chief Financial Officer within 24 hours of receipt.
Include a brief memorandum stating that the gift is pre-decisional with the gift transmittal.
Include any documentation that accompanied the gift.
Mail gift fund checks to the specified USPS address.
Mail gift fund checks to the specified private carrier delivery address.
Transmit wired funds via pay.gov.
Deposit the gift in a suspense account unless it is officially accepted within the 24-hour period.
Perform a balancing test within OFR to determine that the donor and gift are an acceptable source.
Obtain written agreement from the potential donor for conditional monetary gifts that remaining funds of $1,000 or less will be transferred to the unconditional gift account.
Contact the donor to obtain permission in writing to use any amount remaining exceeding the $1,000 threshold.
Coordinate establishing a CAN for the gift upon approval.
Ensure that both a letter of acceptance and a check are present for establishing a new CAN from the CDC Foundation or any other donor.
Notify the CIO that the Office of Budget, OCFO, has approved budget authority and a CAN before committing or obligating funds.
Prepare the letter of acceptance from CDC to the donor stating that the monetary gift is being deposited into the component’s gift fund account.
Deposit the monetary gift into the gift fund account, recording it as conditional or unconditional.
Return any uncashed checks tendered by the donor if the monetary gift is not accepted or the donor refuses to accept CDC terms.
Draw a refund check from the Department of the Treasury if the donor's check has already been deposited.
Thank the donor for non-monetary gifts and inform them that the donation will be considered for acceptance.
Notify the Logistics Management Branch, OSSAM, of proposals for personal property gifts.
Submit a copy of the document accepting the personal property gift to OSSAM.
Create a basic record in the on-line property system upon notification of acceptance of the gift by the CIO property custodian.
Request in writing that the Chief Financial Officer invest excess funds in interest-bearing obligations if excess funds are determined to be $50,000 or more.

preconditions.text: If a CIO makes a determination that there is a potential conflict of interest concern, then the CIO must first consult with OGC.
Before obtaining written agreement from the potential donor, ensure that the monetary gift is conditional.
If you answer was “no” to any questions 1 through 7, or “yes to any questions 8 through 18, then careful scrutiny should be given to any unfavorable responses.

constraints.text: CDC will not accept a gift that is conditioned upon any expenditure that cannot be covered by the gift itself or from income earned from the gift, unless an Act of Congress has approved such expenditure.
Gifts may not be accepted if the conditions placed on the gift create a conflict of interest.
Gifts tendered in order to secure an implied or direct endorsement are not accepted.
Donors from commercial organizations must be advised that the acceptance of a gift does not constitute an implied or direct endorsement.
